title: Regular Expressions Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and patterns for using regular expressions effectively.

commands.description: Matches a Social Security number format.
commands.scenario: Use to validate input when expecting a Social Security number.

commands.description: Searches for lines containing 'error' or 'fail' in a file.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to quickly find error messages in log files.

commands.description: Replaces the word 'cat' with 'dog' globally in a text.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to replace specific words in a document.

commands.description: Matches any whole word in a text.
commands.scenario: Use to identify and extract words from a string.

commands.description: Prints lines starting with 'ERROR' from a file.
commands.scenario: Use to filter log entries that indicate errors.
Use to validate date input in a standard format.

commands.description: Prints lines matching a pattern from a file.
commands.scenario: Use to extract specific lines from a text file based on a pattern.

commands.description: Finds all text files in the current directory and subdirectories.
commands.scenario: Use to locate all text files within a directory tree.

commands.description: Doubles every number found in a file.
commands.scenario: Use to perform arithmetic operations on numbers within a text file.

commands.description: Matches one or more whitespace characters.
commands.scenario: Use to identify and manipulate whitespace in strings.

commands.description: Searches for 'warning' or 'notice' in a file, case-insensitive.
commands.scenario: Use to find non-critical log messages in a case-insensitive manner.

commands.description: Matches a standard email address format.
commands.scenario: Use to validate email addresses in user input.

commands.description: Replaces 'foo' with 'bar' in a file using Vim and saves it.
commands.scenario: Use to perform batch text replacements directly from the command line.
